mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of a standard Turing Machine?
mcqs.explanation: A Turing Machine has a Tape, Read-Write Head, State Register, and Transition Function. It does not use a stack; it has only linear tape memory.

mcqs.question: A Turing Machine is more powerful than a Pushdown Automaton because it can:
mcqs.explanation: Turing Machine can access any cell on the tape at any time (non-linear/random access), unlike PDA which only uses stack (LIFO) access.

mcqs.question: The language accepted by a Turing Machine is called:
mcqs.explanation: Languages accepted by Turing Machines are exactly the recursively enumerable (RE) languages, also known as Turing-recognizable languages.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is true about the Halting Problem?

options.text: It can be solved by a Turing Machine
It is decidable but not enumerable
It is solvable for all inputs

mcqs.explanation: The Halting Problem is undecidable - no algorithm can determine for all program-input pairs whether the program will halt.

mcqs.question: A Universal Turing Machine (UTM) can simulate:
mcqs.explanation: A UTM takes as input the encoding of any TM and its input, then simulates that TM's behavior on the given input.

mcqs.question: In a Turing Machine transition δ(q, a) = (p, b, R), what does 'R' represent?
mcqs.explanation: In transition notation δ(current_state, read_symbol) = (new_state, write_symbol, direction), R means move the head one cell to the right.

mcqs.question: Which of these is the most powerful model of computation?
mcqs.explanation: Turing Machine is the most powerful - it can simulate all other computational models and recognizes the class of all recursively enumerable languages.

mcqs.question: A language is decidable if:

options.text: A TM accepts it but may loop on non-members
A TM halts on all inputs (accepts or rejects)

mcqs.explanation: A language is decidable (recursive) if there exists a TM that halts on all inputs, accepting strings in the language and rejecting strings not in it.

mcqs.question: What is the initial configuration of a Turing Machine with input 'w'?
mcqs.explanation: Initial configuration is (start state, input on tape with blanks on both sides, head at first symbol). Format: (q_start, ☐w☐, 0).

mcqs.question: The Church-Turing Thesis states that:

options.text: Turing machines can solve NP problems efficiently

mcqs.explanation: Church-Turing Thesis states that any effectively calculable/computable function can be computed by a Turing Machine.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT a property of recursively enumerable languages?
mcqs.explanation: Recursively enumerable languages are not closed under complementation. If L is RE, its complement need not be RE.

mcqs.question: A non-deterministic Turing Machine (NTM) is equivalent in power to:
mcqs.explanation: Every NTM can be simulated by a DTM. While NTM may appear more powerful, they are computationally equivalent - both recognize the same class of languages (RE).

mcqs.question: In the acceptance criteria of a TM, rejecting can occur by:

options.text: Entering reject state or looping forever
Only by moving left beyond start

mcqs.explanation: A TM can reject either by explicitly entering a reject state OR by entering an infinite loop (for non-members of the language).

mcqs.question: What is the time complexity of simulating a k-tape TM on a single-tape TM?
mcqs.explanation: Simulating a k-tape TM on a single-tape TM takes O(n²) time. Each step of the k-tape machine may require O(n) passes over the tape.

mcqs.question: Which of the following problems is undecidable?

options.text: Checking if a DFA accepts a string
Checking if two DFAs are equivalent

mcqs.explanation: Post Correspondence Problem (PCP) is undecidable. The other options (string acceptance, DFA equivalence, CFL membership) are all decidable.

mcqs.question: A Turing Machine with a restricted tape (limited to input length) is called:
mcqs.explanation: A Linear Bounded Automaton (LBA) is a restricted TM where the tape is bounded by the length of the input. It recognizes context-sensitive languages.

mcqs.question: If a TM enters its accept state, the computation:

options.text: Must continue until all tape is scanned
Terminates and the input is accepted

mcqs.explanation: When a TM enters its accept state, the computation halts and the input string is accepted. The machine stops further processing.

mcqs.question: The set of all languages that can be recognized by Turing Machines is:
mcqs.explanation: Turing Machines recognize exactly the recursively enumerable (RE) or Turing-recognizable languages, which is the largest class in the Chomsky hierarchy.

mcqs.question: Which theorem proves that the Halting Problem is undecidable?
mcqs.explanation: Rice's Theorem states that any non-trivial property of the language recognized by a TM is undecidable. The Halting Problem is a special case of this.

mcqs.question: The tape in a Turing Machine is assumed to be:

options.text: Finite to the left, infinite to the right

mcqs.explanation: The standard TM model assumes a tape that is infinite (unbounded) in both directions, allowing the head to move indefinitely left or right.
